channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 6.4M cars have defective airbags a decade after initial recall
item.description: More than 6 million cars on United States roads have a dangerous, potentially deadly, piece of equipment inside.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court sends Trump immunity case back to lower court, dimming chance of trial before election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday extended the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss, all but ending prospects the former president could be tried before the November election.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for a North Dakota truck stop in a new blow to federal regulations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court opened the door Monday to new, broad challenges to regulations long after they take effect, the third blow in a week to federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 kids taken by dad considered 'missing and endangered': Deputies
item.description: Authorities in Maury County are searching for three children who are believed to be "missing and endangered" after they were removed from their father's custody.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, NewsNation

item.title: Michael J. Fox rocks out with Coldplay at Glastonbury
item.description: Fans at Glastonbury Festival were surprised by a performance by Marty McFly. On Saturday, Michael J. Fox joined Coldplay onstage during their set at the United Kingdom festival. The band's frontman Chris Martin welcomed the actor to the front of the stage in front of 100,000 fans.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: In a proud and troubled UK town, voters wonder whether their election choice will make a difference
item.description: HARTLEPOOL, England (AP) — A lot of politicians have promised change to voters in Hartlepool, a wind-whipped port town in northeast England. For decades, Labour Party representatives said they would fight for working people, even as well-paid industrial jobs disappeared.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Turkey's president accuses opposition of stoking racism after anti-Syrian rioting erupts
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan accused opposition parties of stoking xenophobia and racism on Monday, a day after residents in a neighborhood in central Turkey set Syrian-owned shops on fire.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: French far right works to turn election win into power. Its rivals want to stop it in decisive vote
item.description: PARIS (AP) — With the ultimate outcome still up in the air, France's fiercely anti-immigration National Rally and opponents of the long-taboo far-right party scrambled on Monday to capitalize on an indecisive first round of voting the day before in surprise legislative elections.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, SYLVIE CORBET and BARBARA SURK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hurricane Beryl takes aim at southeastern Caribbean as a powerful Category 4 storm
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Hurricane Beryl began pounding the southeast Caribbean on Monday as a powerful Category 4 storm after becoming the earliest storm of that strength to form in the Atlantic, fueled by record warm waters.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US military heightens the security alert at European bases due to a combination of threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has raised the security protection measures it is taking at its bases throughout Europe, asking service members to be more vigilant and keep a lower profile due to a combination of threats it is seeing across the region. U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Which states could have abortion on the ballot in 2024?
item.description: With Friday's announcement in Nevada that voters will decide a ballot question in November on enshrining abortion rights in the state constitution, five states now have abortion questions set for referendums this year. And more could join them. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Magic Johnson admires how Caitlin Clark and Angel Reese deal with the hype while uplifting the WNBA
item.description: Magic Johnson admires how Caitlin Clark and Angel Reese are handling the glaring spotlight of expectations and living up to the moments with record-setting performances while uplifting a league. Johnson knows firsthand what that's like; he and Larry Bird did it 45 years ago.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court sends Trump immunity case back to lower court, dimming chance of trial before election
item.description: In a historic 6-3 ruling, the justices said for the first time that former presidents have absolute immunity from prosecution for their official acts and no immunity for unofficial acts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: California prisoner who spent 13 years on the run after escaping prison camp is recaptured
item.description: Eduardo Hernandez, who has spent nearly 13 years on the run after escaping a prison camp in California's Bay Area, has been tracked down in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inside VidCon 2024: Here's the secret to becoming a social media star
item.description: VidCon is a place where creators, their fans, brands and merch collide. "Hollywood is no longer just Hollywood," explains Sarah Tortoreti, VidCon's SVP of Marketing and Communications.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: After 32 years as a progressive voice for LGBTQ Jews, Rabbi Sharon Kleinbaum heads into retirement
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For more than three decades, Rabbi Sharon Kleinbaum has led the nation’s largest LGBTQ+ synagogue through the myriad ups and downs of the modern gay-rights movement — through the AIDS crisis, the murder of Matthew Shepard, the historic civil-rights advances that included marriage equ
item.dc:creator: KEN SWEET, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors target West Texas meth dealers
item.description: Separate grand juries this week charged four men and three women with conspiring to distribute methamphetamine in the oil-rich Midland, Texas, region.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Live updates: Supreme Court rules Trump has criminal immunity for official acts
item.description: In a monumental ruling, the Supreme Court ruled that former presidents enjoy a presumption criminal immunity for official acts while in the White House, handing a win to former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, ABC 4

item.title: US Supreme Court Latest: Court sends Trump’s immunity case back to lower court
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court justices have sent Donald Trump’s immunity case back to a lower court in Washington, dimming the prospect of a pre-election trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Voters kick all the Republican women out of the South Carolina Senate
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — The only three Republican women in the South Carolina Senate took on their party and stopped a total abortion ban from passing in their state last year. In return, they lost their jobs. Voters removed Sens.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon will report to federal prison to serve 4-month sentence on contempt charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Longtime Trump ally Steve Bannon is scheduled to report to a federal prison in Connecticut on Monday to serve a four-month sentence on contempt charges for defying a subpoena in the congressional investigation into the U.S. Capitol attack.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden campaign email details how to defend president’s 'rough' debate performance and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Beryl strengthens into Category 4 storm, Tropical Depression Chris bringing heavy rain, flooding to Mexico
item.description: The National Hurricane Center is tracking Hurricane Beryl, Tropical Storm Chris and a new tropical wave.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Brieskorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jocelyn Nungaray murder: Texas county dubbed 'sanctuary for criminals' as Dem DA tries to shift blame
item.description: Harris County District Attorney Kim Ogg blamed the "broken" immigration system as two illegal immigrants were accused of killing 12-year-old Jocelyn Nungaray in Houston, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah family desperate for answers after man vanishes before lunch date with mom: 'He matters'
item.description: Justin Hooiman vanished after making plans to meet his mom for lunch in Salt Lake City in November 2017 – now police are looking into a potential homicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican JD Vance journeys from 'Hillbilly Elegy' memoirist to US senator to VP contender
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — It was March 2022 and Senate candidate JD Vance was standing under hot lights in a Cleveland television studio debating four fellow Republicans on whether the U.S. should support a no-fly zone over Ukraine, not a month into its grinding war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden’s future, Trump’s immunity dominate presidential campaign
item.description: President Biden’s family urged him to stay in the race for reelection, a source confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Family demands accountability for NY police killing of 13-year-old boy. Police said he aimed BB gun
item.description: On Wednesday, Nyah Mway finished middle school in the central New York city where his family moved about a decade ago as refugees from Myanmar, relatives said.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump immunity decision in balance as Supreme Court caps term
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to rule on Monday whether former President Trump has immunity from criminal prosecution, a monumental decision that comes just days after the court dropped a decision throwing into doubt charges against hundreds of Jan. 6 rioters and Trump himself.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US wants Boeing to plead guilty to fraud over fatal crashes, lawyers say
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department plans to propose that Boeing plead guilty to criminal fraud in connection with two deadly plane crashes involving its 737 Max jetliners.
item.dc:creator: David Koenig, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Teen with pellet gun fatally shot by police: Body cam footage released as community calls for accountability
item.description: Police say the 13-year-old aimed an object - later identified as a pellet gun - at them before he was fatally shot. The boy's family is now calling for the officers to be "put in jail.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Donovan, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Having a child? These are the popular baby-naming trends to know — or avoid
item.description: Couples struggling to find the perfect name for their baby have plenty of resources to help them gather a list of possible choices. But as it just so happens, many of these parents-to-be are seeking the same types of names.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Missing mom, 2 daughters may have been 'coerced to travel to Mexico': Kansas authorities
item.description: Investigators believe all three may have been coerced into traveling to "a religious rehabilitation facility" in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Carina Branson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fire captain deployed to Ground Zero on 9/11 dies in Colorado rafting mishap
item.description: A Utah fire captain who was deployed to Ground Zero after the 9/11 terrorist attacks was killed in a rafting accident in Colorado last week, officials have confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Exotic mammal native to Central and South America spotted at Washington rest stop
item.description: Also referred to as “honey bears," the species is usually found in tropical rainforests from southern Mexico to Brazil.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, WGN 9

item.title: Tropical Storm Beryl forecast to become a major hurricane as it approaches the southeast Caribbean
item.description: Tropical Storm Beryl chugged toward the southeast Caribbean on Saturday as forecasters warned it was expected to strengthen into a dangerous major hurricane before reaching Barbados late Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Danica Coto, WGN 9

item.title: Animal rescuers try to keep dozens of dolphins away from Cape Cod shallows after mass stranding
item.description: WELLFLEET, Mass. (AP) — Animal rescuers were trying to keep dozens of dolphins away from shallow waters around Cape Cod on Saturday after 125 of the creatures stranded themselves a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
